class.name: WifiManager
class.brief_description: Cross-platform Wi-Fi scan, connect, disconnect, and connectivity monitoring.
class.description: [WifiManager] is the main GDExtension node for WifiGD. Add it to your scene tree or register [code]wifi_manager_autoload.tscn[/code] as an autoload singleton to scan for networks, connect to open or WPA2-PSK networks, disconnect, and read adapter and connectivity state. Blocking methods run on a worker thread pool where possible; prefer the [code]*_async[/code] variants and signals during gameplay. [b]Supported platforms:[/b] Windows (WLAN API), Linux (NetworkManager / libnm). macOS is stubbed. [b]WifiNetwork dictionary[/b] fields in scan results: [codeblock lang=text] ssid — Network name bssid — Access point MAC address signal_strength — Normalized signal 0–100 is_secured — Whether a password is required is_connected — Whether this AP is the active association security_type — e.g. open, wpa2, connected frequency_mhz — Center frequency when available channel — Wi-Fi channel when available band — e.g. 2.4, 5, 6 adapter_id — Adapter that observed this network [/codeblock] [b]NetworkAdapter dictionary[/b] fields: [codeblock lang=text] id — Platform adapter id (Windows GUID, Linux wlan0, etc.) name — Friendly interface name description — Optional description mac_address — Hardware MAC ip_address — Current IPv4 when assigned gateway — Default gateway when known dns_primary — Primary DNS when known type — wifi, ethernet, virtual, other, unknown is_up — Administratively up is_connected — Has an active link [/codeblock] [b]Connectivity dictionary[/b] fields: [codeblock lang=text] state — disconnected, connecting, connected, failed is_wifi_connected — Associated with Wi-Fi connected_ssid — Active SSID local_ip — Assigned IPv4 gateway — Default gateway dns_primary — Primary DNS has_internet — Best-effort reachability hint [/codeblock] [b]WifiRadioState dictionary[/b] (from [method get_wifi_radio_state]): [codeblock lang=text] enabled — Effective radio on (matches [method is_wifi_enabled]) software_enabled — OS/software Wi-Fi toggle (NM WirelessEnabled on Linux) hardware_enabled — Hardware rfkill not blocking (NM WirelessHardwareEnabled on Linux) can_toggle — UI hint: true when hardware is not rfkill-blocked permission — Linux polkit hint: yes, auth, no, or unknown (informational) [/codeblock] [b]Wi-Fi radio:[/b] Prefer [method set_wifi_enabled_async] and [signal wifi_radio_set_completed] during gameplay. On Linux, WifiGD always attempts the NetworkManager toggle and reports success when the radio state actually changes (no sudo required when NM allows it for the session user). [code]permission[/code] is a hint only — Godot cannot show polkit dialogs, so [code]auth[/code] may still fail without a desktop polkit agent.

method.name: is_wifi_enabled
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the Wi-Fi radio is effectively on. On Linux this is the software toggle [b]and[/b] hardware not rfkill-blocked (same as [code]enabled[/code] in [method get_wifi_radio_state]). Updated by internal polling and after successful radio changes.

method.name: set_wifi_enabled
method.description: [b]Synchronous.[/b] Enables or disables the Wi-Fi radio and blocks until the OS request completes (up to ~30s on Linux). May require administrator privileges on Windows or polkit / elevation on Linux. Returns [code]false[/code] on failure; see [method get_last_error]. Does not emit [signal wifi_radio_set_completed] — use [method set_wifi_enabled_async] for signal-based UI. On success, [signal wifi_enabled_changed] is emitted when polling detects the new state.

method.name: set_wifi_enabled_async
method.description: Starts an asynchronous Wi-Fi radio enable/disable on a worker thread. Result arrives via [signal wifi_radio_set_completed]. Only one radio operation runs at a time; a second call while busy emits [signal wifi_radio_set_completed] with [code]ERR_BUSY[/code]. On Linux, requires NetworkManager and polkit permission [code]enable-disable-wifi[/code]; Godot cannot show polkit authorization dialogs — configure policy or run elevated if toggles fail without a desktop polkit agent.

method.name: get_wifi_radio_state
method.description: Returns a [b]WifiRadioState[/b] dictionary (see class description): [code]enabled[/code], [code]software_enabled[/code], [code]hardware_enabled[/code], [code]can_toggle[/code], and [code]permission[/code] ([code]yes[/code], [code]auth[/code], [code]no[/code], or [code]unknown[/code]). Use to decide whether to show or disable a Wi-Fi radio switch before calling [method set_wifi_enabled_async].

method.name: scan_wifi_networks
method.param: Target adapter, or empty for the default Wi-Fi adapter.
method.description: [b]Synchronous.[/b] Scans for nearby networks and blocks until complete. Prefer [method scan_wifi_networks_async] on the main thread during gameplay.

method.name: scan_wifi_networks_async
method.param: Target adapter, or empty for the default Wi-Fi adapter.
method.description: Starts an asynchronous scan. Results arrive via [signal scan_completed].

method.name: connect_to_wifi
method.param: WPA2-PSK password; leave empty for open networks.
Target adapter, or empty for the default Wi-Fi adapter.
method.description: [b]Synchronous.[/b] Connects to a network and blocks until the request finishes. Prefer [method connect_to_wifi_async].

method.name: connect_to_wifi_async
method.param: WPA2-PSK password; leave empty for open networks.
Target adapter, or empty for the default Wi-Fi adapter.
method.description: Starts an asynchronous connect. Result arrives via [signal connect_completed].

method.name: disconnect_from_wifi
method.param: Target adapter, or empty for the default Wi-Fi adapter.
method.description: [b]Synchronous.[/b] Disconnects from the current Wi-Fi network. Prefer [method disconnect_from_wifi_async].

method.name: disconnect_from_wifi_async
method.param: Target adapter, or empty for the default Wi-Fi adapter.
method.description: Starts an asynchronous disconnect. Result arrives via [signal disconnect_completed].

method.name: get_connectivity_info
method.description: Returns a fresh connectivity snapshot (see class description for fields).

method.name: get_cached_connectivity
method.description: Returns the last cached connectivity snapshot updated by internal polling (~every 2 seconds).

method.name: get_connection_state
method.description: Returns the cached connection state constant: [constant DISCONNECTED], [constant CONNECTING], [constant CONNECTED], or [constant FAILED].

method.name: get_network_adapters
method.description: [b]Synchronous.[/b] Returns the current list of network adapters.

method.name: get_cached_adapters
method.description: Returns the last cached adapter list.

method.name: fetch_adapters_async
method.description: Refreshes adapters asynchronously. Result arrives via [signal adapters_updated].

method.name: get_last_error
method.description: Returns a short, user-facing message for the last failure. Detailed OS errors are also logged to the Godot Output panel.

signal.name: scan_completed
signal.param: Friendly status or error text for UI logs.

signal.name: connectivity_changed
signal.description: Emitted when connectivity state changes (polled automatically while the node is in the tree).

signal.name: connection_state_changed
signal.param: One of [constant DISCONNECTED], [constant CONNECTING], [constant CONNECTED], [constant FAILED].
Active SSID, or empty when disconnected.
signal.description: Emitted when connection state or active SSID changes.

signal.name: wifi_enabled_changed
signal.description: Emitted when the effective Wi-Fi radio state changes (polled automatically ~every 2 seconds while the node is in the tree, and after a successful async toggle). Matches [method is_wifi_enabled] / [code]enabled[/code] in [method get_wifi_radio_state].

signal.name: wifi_radio_set_completed
signal.param: Godot [enum Error] code ([code]OK[/code] on success, [code]ERR_CANT_CONNECT[/code] on OS failure, [code]ERR_BUSY[/code] if another radio change is in progress).
Friendly status or error text for UI logs.
signal.description: Emitted when [method set_wifi_enabled_async] finishes. Check [code]error == OK[/code] before syncing UI; on failure [code]message[/code] often indicates polkit denial, rfkill, or timeout. Does not fire for [method set_wifi_enabled] (sync).
